guidelines.title: PCOS screening context
guidelines.snippet: Elevated LH:FSH ratio with hyperandrogenism and oligo-anovulation is a commonly used PCOS screening pattern — formal diagnosis requires clinical evaluation.

guidelines.title: Insulin resistance · lifestyle first
guidelines.snippet: For insulin resistance patterns, low-GI dietary pattern and resistance exercise are first-line before medication discussion.

guidelines.title: Vitamin D insufficiency
guidelines.snippet: Vitamin D insufficiency is associated with insulin sensitivity and mood burden — repletion with retesting is standard practice.

guidelines.title: Myo-inositol evidence
guidelines.snippet: Myo-inositol has the strongest supplement evidence base for PCOS-related insulin sensitivity and LH:FSH improvement — discuss with clinician if on metformin.

guidelines.title: PCOS screening context
guidelines.snippet: Elevated LH:FSH ratio with hyperandrogenism and oligo-anovulation is a commonly used PCOS screening pattern — formal diagnosis requires clinical evaluation.

guidelines.title: Insulin resistance · lifestyle first
guidelines.snippet: For insulin resistance patterns, low-GI dietary pattern and resistance exercise are first-line before medication discussion.

guidelines.title: Vitamin D insufficiency
guidelines.snippet: Vitamin D insufficiency is associated with insulin sensitivity and mood burden — repletion with retesting is standard practice.

guidelines.title: Myo-inositol evidence
guidelines.snippet: Myo-inositol has the strongest supplement evidence base for PCOS-related insulin sensitivity and LH:FSH improvement — discuss with clinician if on metformin.

foods.title: Low-GI protein at every meal

foods.title: Oily fish 3× per week
foods.why: Supports triglycerides and inflammatory load

foods.title: Reduce dairy + high-GI carbs on inflammation days
foods.why: Logged triggers correlate with symptom spikes

foods.title: Iron-aware meals (leafy greens + vitamin C pairing)
foods.why: Ferritin low-normal — food-first before supplementing iron

foods.title: Spearmint tea (2 cups daily)
foods.why: Evidence for lowering free testosterone in PCOS patterns

foods.title: Magnesium-rich foods (pumpkin seeds, leafy greens)

supplements.evidence: Strong PCOS insulin + LH:FSH evidence base
supplements.note: Discuss with clinician if already on metformin

supplements.name: Vitamin D3 + K2
supplements.evidence: Replete insufficiency — linked to insulin, mood, bone

supplements.name: Magnesium glycinate
supplements.evidence: Sleep, glucose handling, cramping — low risk

supplements.name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)

supplements.evidence: May improve insulin sensitivity and SHBG
supplements.note: Avoid high doses with asthma history
